    if info["flush_draw"] and info["straight_draw"] != "none":
        quality = max(quality, flush_quality + straight_quality + 0.04)
    if len(public_cards) == 3:
        quality += 0.025 * info["overcards"]
    elif info["overcards"] >= 2:
        quality += 0.015

    if info["combo_draw"]:
        info["type"] = "combo_draw"
        info["fold_threshold_delta"] = 0.07
        info["size_bonus"] = 0.06
    elif info["nut_flush_draw"]:
        info["type"] = "nut_flush_draw"
        info["fold_threshold_delta"] = 0.05
        info["size_bonus"] = 0.04
    elif info["flush_draw"]:
        info["type"] = "flush_draw"
        if info["near_nut_flush_draw"]:
            info["fold_threshold_delta"] = 0.04
            info["size_bonus"] = 0.035
        elif info["high_flush_draw"]:
            info["fold_threshold_delta"] = 0.03
            info["size_bonus"] = 0.025
        else:
            info["fold_threshold_delta"] = 0.01 if info["flush_draw_rank"] >= 10 else 0.0
            info["size_bonus"] = 0.015
    elif info["straight_draw"] == "open_ended":
        info["type"] = "open_ended_straight_draw"
        info["fold_threshold_delta"] = 0.03
        info["size_bonus"] = 0.02
    elif info["straight_draw"] == "double_gutshot":
        info["type"] = "double_gutshot"
        info["fold_threshold_delta"] = 0.02
        info["size_bonus"] = 0.01
    elif info["straight_draw"] == "gutshot":
        info["type"] = "gutshot"
        info["fold_threshold_delta"] = -0.03
        info["size_bonus"] = -0.02

    info["quality"] = clamp(quality, 0.0, 0.35)
    info["semi_bluff"] = (
        info["combo_draw"]
        or info["nut_flush_draw"]
        or info["straight_draw"] in ("open_ended", "double_gutshot")
        or (info["flush_draw"] and info["quality"] >= 0.16)
        or (info["straight_draw"] == "gutshot" and info["overcards"] >= 1 and info["quality"] >= 0.13)
    )
    return info


def draw_potential(hole_cards, public_cards):
    return draw_profile(hole_cards, public_cards)["quality"]


def draw_call_margin(draw_info, board_texture, round_idx, spot_info):
    if draw_info is None or draw_info["type"] == "none":
        return 0.0

    margin = 0.0
    draw_type = draw_info["type"]
    size_bucket = bet_size_bucket(spot_info["last_raise_pot_ratio"])

    if draw_type == "combo_draw":
        margin -= 0.035
    elif draw_type == "nut_flush_draw":
        margin -= 0.025
    elif draw_type == "open_ended_straight_draw":
        margin -= 0.012
    elif draw_type == "double_gutshot":
        margin -= 0.006
    elif draw_type == "gutshot":
        margin += 0.040
    elif draw_type == "flush_draw" and not draw_info["nut_flush_draw"]:
        if draw_info.get("near_nut_flush_draw", False):
            margin -= 0.010
        elif draw_info.get("high_flush_draw", False):
            margin += 0.004
        else:
            margin += 0.020

    if (
        draw_type == "flush_draw"
        and draw_info.get("high_flush_draw", False)
        and size_bucket in ("small", "medium")
        and spot_info.get("has_position", False)
        and (board_texture is None or not board_texture["paired"])
    ):
        margin -= 0.006

    if board_texture is not None:
        if board_texture["paired"] and draw_info["flush_draw"] and not draw_info["nut_flush_draw"]:
            margin += 0.030
        if board_texture["flush_pressure"] >= 0.75 and draw_type == "open_ended_straight_draw":
            margin += 0.008

    if round_idx == 2:
        if draw_type == "gutshot":
            margin += 0.020
        elif draw_type == "flush_draw":
            if draw_info.get("near_nut_flush_draw", False) and size_bucket != "large" and (board_texture is None or not board_texture["paired"]):
                margin += 0.000
            elif draw_info.get("high_flush_draw", False) and size_bucket == "small" and (board_texture is None or not board_texture["paired"]):
                margin += 0.006
            else:
                margin += 0.020
    elif round_idx == 3:
        margin += 0.050

    if size_bucket == "large":
        if draw_type == "gutshot":
            margin += 0.018
        elif draw_type == "flush_draw":
            if draw_info.get("near_nut_flush_draw", False):
                margin += 0.006
            elif draw_info.get("high_flush_draw", False):
                margin += 0.012
            else:
                margin += 0.018

    return clamp(margin, -0.04, 0.08)


def made_flush_profile(hole_cards, public_cards, board_texture=None):
    info = {
        "is_flush": False,
        "flush_suit": None,
        "hole_flush_ranks": [],
        "board_flush_ranks": [],
        "high_hole_rank": 0,
        "better_unseen_ranks": 0,
        "nut_like": False,
        "repressure_continue": False,
    }

    if len(public_cards) < 3:
        return info

    if board_texture is None:
        board_texture = board_texture_profile(public_cards)

    score = evaluate_best(hole_cards + public_cards)
    if score[0] != 5:
        return info

    suit_counts = {}
    for card in hole_cards + public_cards:
        suit = card_suit(card)
        suit_counts[suit] = suit_counts.get(suit, 0) + 1

    flush_suits = [suit for suit, count in suit_counts.items() if count >= 5]
    if not flush_suits:
        return info

    flush_suit = max(
        flush_suits,
        key=lambda suit: sorted(
            (card_number(card) for card in hole_cards + public_cards if card_suit(card) == suit),
            reverse=True,
        )[:5],
    )
    hole_flush_ranks = sorted(
        (card_number(card) for card in hole_cards if card_suit(card) == flush_suit),
        reverse=True,
    )
    board_flush_ranks = sorted(
        (card_number(card) for card in public_cards if card_suit(card) == flush_suit),
        reverse=True,
    )

    if not hole_flush_ranks:
        return info

    high_hole = hole_flush_ranks[0]
    seen_flush_ranks = set(hole_flush_ranks + board_flush_ranks)
    better_unseen = [rank for rank in range(high_hole + 1, 15) if rank not in seen_flush_ranks]

    info["is_flush"] = True
    info["flush_suit"] = flush_suit
    info["hole_flush_ranks"] = hole_flush_ranks
    info["board_flush_ranks"] = board_flush_ranks
    info["high_hole_rank"] = high_hole
    info["better_unseen_ranks"] = len(better_unseen)
    info["nut_like"] = len(better_unseen) == 0

    three_flush_board = len(board_flush_ranks) == 3
    high_private_flush = high_hole >= 11 and len(better_unseen) <= 2
    info["repressure_continue"] = (
        not board_texture["paired"]
        and three_flush_board
        and high_private_flush
    )
    return info


def blocker_bluff_profile(hole_cards, public_cards, pair_profile=None, board_texture=None):
    info = {
        "eligible": False,
        "score": 0.0,
        "type": "none",
    }

    if len(public_cards) < 3:
        return info

    if pair_profile is None:
        pair_profile = pair_board_profile(hole_cards, public_cards)
    if board_texture is None:
        board_texture = board_texture_profile(public_cards)

    score = evaluate_best(hole_cards + public_cards)
    if score[0] >= 1 and pair_profile["pair_type"] != "board_pair":
        return info

    board_suits = [card_suit(card) for card in public_cards]
    suit_counts = {}
    for suit in board_suits:
        suit_counts[suit] = suit_counts.get(suit, 0) + 1
    target_suit = max(suit_counts, key=suit_counts.get)
    max_board_suit = suit_counts[target_suit]

    blocker_score = 0.0
    bluff_type = "none"
    suited_hole_ranks = sorted(
        (card_number(card) for card in hole_cards if card_suit(card) == target_suit),
        reverse=True,
    )
    if max_board_suit >= 3 and suited_hole_ranks:
        high_blocker = suited_hole_ranks[0]
        if high_blocker == 14:
            blocker_score += 0.24
            bluff_type = "flush_ace_blocker"
        elif high_blocker == 13:
            blocker_score += 0.18
            bluff_type = "flush_king_blocker"
        elif high_blocker == 12:
            blocker_score += 0.11
            bluff_type = "flush_queen_blocker"

    hole_ranks = [card_number(card) for card in hole_cards]
    if board_texture["paired"] and max(hole_ranks) >= 13:
        blocker_score += 0.05
        if bluff_type == "none":
            bluff_type = "paired_board_blocker"
    if board_texture["high_card"] >= 12 and 14 in hole_ranks:
        blocker_score += 0.04
        if bluff_type == "none":
            bluff_type = "ace_high_blocker"
    if board_texture["straight_pressure"] >= 0.65 and max(hole_ranks) >= max(10, board_texture["high_card"] - 1):
        blocker_score += 0.04
        if bluff_type == "none":
            bluff_type = "straight_pressure_blocker"

    info["score"] = blocker_score
    info["type"] = bluff_type
    info["eligible"] = blocker_score >= 0.14
    return info


def allow_low_frequency_blocker_bluff(req, hole_cards, public_cards, blocker_profile, round_idx):
    if not blocker_profile["eligible"]:
        return False

    hand_idx = get_hand_index(req) or 0
    token = (sum(hole_cards) * 7 + sum(public_cards) * 11 + hand_idx * 13 + round_idx * 17) % 100
    threshold = int(clamp(blocker_profile["score"] * 35.0, 5.0, 18.0))
    return token < threshold


def nutted_risk_profile(hole_cards, public_cards, pair_profile=None, board_texture=None, value_profile=None, paired_board_profile=None):
    info = {
        "risk": 0.0,
        "label": "none",
        "vulnerable": False,
    }

    if len(public_cards) < 3:
        return info

    if pair_profile is None:
        pair_profile = pair_board_profile(hole_cards, public_cards)
    if board_texture is None:
        board_texture = board_texture_profile(public_cards)
    if paired_board_profile is None:
        paired_board_profile = paired_board_outcome_profile(hole_cards, public_cards)
    if value_profile is None:
        value_profile = value_hand_tier(hole_cards, public_cards, pair_profile, board_texture, paired_board_profile)

    score = evaluate_best(hole_cards + public_cards)
    hand_class = score[0]
    board_ranks = [card_number(card) for card in public_cards]
    hole_ranks = [card_number(card) for card in hole_cards]

    risk = 0.0
    label = "none"

    if hand_class == 6:
        if len(set(board_ranks)) <= 3 and score[1] < max(board_ranks):
            risk += 0.045
            label = "low_full_house"
    elif hand_class == 5:
        flush_profile = made_flush_profile(hole_cards, public_cards, board_texture)
        if flush_profile["nut_like"]:
            label = "nut_like_flush"
        elif flush_profile["high_hole_rank"] >= 12 and flush_profile["better_unseen_ranks"] <= 1:
            risk += 0.010
            label = "near_nut_flush"
        elif flush_profile["high_hole_rank"] >= 10:
            risk += 0.025
            label = "medium_flush"
        else:
            risk += 0.050
            label = "weak_flush"
        if board_texture["paired"]:
            risk += 0.03
            label += "_paired_board"
    elif hand_class == 4:
        if board_texture["flush_pressure"] >= 0.75:
            risk += 0.04
            label = "straight_on_flush_board"
        if board_texture["paired"]:
            risk += 0.03
            label = "straight_on_paired_board"
    elif hand_class == 3:
        if board_texture["paired"]:
            if paired_board_profile["strengthened"]:
                risk += 0.02
                label = paired_board_profile["label"]
            else:
                risk += 0.04
                label = "trips_on_paired_board"
        if score[1] < max(board_ranks):
            risk += 0.02
    elif hand_class == 2 and board_texture["paired"]:
        if paired_board_profile["board_two_pair"]:
            risk += 0.07
            label = paired_board_profile["label"]
        elif paired_board_profile["fragile_two_pair"]:
            risk += 0.08
            label = paired_board_profile["label"]
        elif paired_board_profile["weakened"]:
            risk += 0.06
            label = paired_board_profile["label"]
        elif paired_board_profile["strengthened"]:
            risk += 0.03
            label = paired_board_profile["label"]
        else:
            risk += 0.05
            label = "two_pair_on_paired_board"
    elif hand_class == 1 and value_profile["tier"] in ("strong", "thin"):
        if board_texture["flush_pressure"] >= 1.0:
            risk += 0.03
            label = "pair_on_four_flush"
        if board_texture["straight_pressure"] >= 1.0:
            risk += 0.02
            label = "pair_on_four_straight"

    info["risk"] = clamp(risk, 0.0, 0.14)
    info["label"] = label
    info["vulnerable"] = info["risk"] >= 0.04
    return info


--- bots/claude_v6/main.py ---
"""
Bot 6 — slim entry point.
"""
import json
import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))

from state import reconstruct_state, infer_remaining_hands_from_requests
from strategy import get_action


def sanitize_action(action, state, my_chips):
    if state["opponent_allin"]:
        return action if action in (-1, -2) else -1

    if state["to_call"] >= my_chips:
        return -2 if action == -2 else -1

    if action > 0:
        min_raise = state.get("min_raise_action", state["round_raise"])
        if action >= my_chips:
            return -2
        if action < min_raise or action <= state["to_call"]:
            return 0

    if action == 0 and state["to_call"] > 0:
        return 0

    return action


def main():
    payload = json.loads(input())
    requests = payload["requests"]
    req = dict(requests[-1])
    if "remaining_hands" not in req:
        req["remaining_hands"] = infer_remaining_hands_from_requests(requests)
    action = get_action(req, requests)
    state = reconstruct_state(req)
    action = sanitize_action(action, state, req["my_chips"])
    print(json.dumps({"response": int(action)}))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()


--- bots/claude_v6/tournament.py ---
"""
Tournament-level logic: lock-win detection, match pressure, anti-lock pressure.
"""
from constants import N_PLAYERS, BIG_BLIND, LOCK_WIN_MARGIN
from card_utils import clamp, next_player
from state import (
    get_remaining_hands,
    reconstruct_state,
    forced_fold_loss_bound,
)


def should_lock_win(req, state, my_id):
    total_win_chips = req.get("total_win_chips", [0] * N_PLAYERS)
    if len(total_win_chips) <= my_id:
        return False

    try:
        lead = int(total_win_chips[my_id])
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return False

    remaining_hands = get_remaining_hands(req)
    if remaining_hands is None:
        return lead >= LOCK_WIN_MARGIN

    max_forced_loss = forced_fold_loss_bound(req, state, my_id, remaining_hands)
    if max_forced_loss is None:
        return lead >= LOCK_WIN_MARGIN
    return lead > max_forced_loss


def opponent_can_lock_win(req, my_id):
    opponent_id = next_player(my_id, 1)
    total_win_chips = req.get("total_win_chips", [0] * N_PLAYERS)
    if len(total_win_chips) <= opponent_id:
        return False

    try:
        lead = int(total_win_chips[opponent_id])
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return False

    remaining_hands = get_remaining_hands(req)
    if remaining_hands is None:
        return lead >= LOCK_WIN_MARGIN

    state = reconstruct_state(req)
    max_forced_loss = forced_fold_loss_bound(req, state, opponent_id, remaining_hands)
    if max_forced_loss is None:
        return lead >= LOCK_WIN_MARGIN
    return lead > max_forced_loss + BIG_BLIND


def fold_gives_opponent_lock(req, state, my_id):
    opponent_id = next_player(my_id, 1)
    total_win_chips = req.get("total_win_chips", [0] * N_PLAYERS)
    if len(total_win_chips) <= opponent_id:
        return False

    remaining_hands = get_remaining_hands(req)
    if remaining_hands is None or remaining_hands <= 1:
        return False

    try:
        opponent_lead = int(total_win_chips[opponent_id])
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return False

    opponent_lead_after_fold = opponent_lead + state["committed"][my_id]
    max_forced_loss = forced_fold_loss_bound(req, state, opponent_id, remaining_hands)
    if max_forced_loss is None:
        return opponent_lead_after_fold >= LOCK_WIN_MARGIN

    future_forced_loss = max(0, max_forced_loss - state["committed"][opponent_id])
    return opponent_lead_after_fold > future_forced_loss


def match_risk_adjustment(req, my_id, remaining_hands):
    total_win_chips = req.get("total_win_chips", [0] * N_PLAYERS)
    if len(total_win_chips) <= my_id:
        return 0.0
    if remaining_hands is None or remaining_hands <= 0:
        return 0.0

    lead = total_win_chips[my_id]
    scale = max(1.0, remaining_hands * BIG_BLIND * 5.0)
    if lead >= 0:
        return min(0.05, lead / scale)
    return -min(0.05, (-lead) / (scale * 0.85))


def match_pressure_profile(req, my_id, remaining_hands):
    profile = {
        "protect": 0.0,
        "chase": 0.0,
        "threshold_delta": 0.0,
        "sizing_delta": 0.0,
        "open_delta": 0.0,
        "bluff_delta": 0.0,
    }

    total_win_chips = req.get("total_win_chips", [0] * N_PLAYERS)
    if len(total_win_chips) <= my_id or remaining_hands is None or remaining_hands <= 0:
        return profile

    try:
        lead = int(total_win_chips[my_id])
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return profile

    hands = max(1, int(remaining_hands))
    late_factor = clamp((12 - hands) / 10.0, 0.0, 1.0)
    if late_factor <= 0.0:
        return profile

    behind_per_hand = max(0.0, -lead) / hands
    ahead_per_hand = max(0.0, lead) / hands

    chase = clamp((behind_per_hand - 0.8 * BIG_BLIND) / (3.5 * BIG_BLIND), 0.0, 1.0)
    protect = clamp((ahead_per_hand - 0.8 * BIG_BLIND) / (3.0 * BIG_BLIND), 0.0, 1.0)
    chase *= late_factor
    protect *= late_factor
    if not (hands <= 8 and lead < -8 * BIG_BLIND):
        chase = min(chase, 0.25)

    profile["protect"] = protect
    profile["chase"] = chase
    profile["threshold_delta"] = 0.055 * protect - 0.055 * chase
    profile["sizing_delta"] = -0.10 * protect + 0.16 * chase
    profile["open_delta"] = 0.020 * protect - 0.030 * chase
    profile["bluff_delta"] = -0.08 * protect + 0.10 * chase
    return profile


def apply_anti_lock_pressure(match_profile):
    match_profile["protect"] = 0.0
    match_profile["chase"] = max(match_profile["chase"], 0.90)
    match_profile["threshold_delta"] = min(match_profile["threshold_delta"], -0.075)
    match_profile["sizing_delta"] = max(match_profile["sizing_delta"], 0.18)
    match_profile["open_delta"] = min(match_profile["open_delta"], -0.045)
    match_profile["bluff_delta"] = max(match_profile["bluff_delta"], 0.13)
    return match_profile


def anti_lock_continue_floor(pot_odds, round_idx, value_profile, draw_info, made_strength):
    discount = 0.07 + 0.025 * max(0, round_idx)

    if value_profile is not None:
        if value_profile.get("tier") == "nut":
            discount += 0.12
        elif value_profile.get("tier") == "strong":
            discount += 0.08
        elif value_profile.get("tier") == "thin":
            discount += 0.035

    if draw_info is not None:
        if draw_info.get("type") in ("combo_draw", "nut_flush_draw"):
            discount += 0.05
        elif draw_info.get("semi_bluff"):
            discount += 0.03

    if made_strength < 0.18 and (draw_info is None or draw_info.get("quality", 0.0) < 0.08):
        discount -= 0.04

    return max(0.08, pot_odds - discount)


def anti_lock_can_continue(anti_lock_pressure, win_rate, pot_odds, round_idx, value_profile, draw_info, made_strength):
    if not anti_lock_pressure:
        return False
    return win_rate >= anti_lock_continue_floor(pot_odds, round_idx, value_profile, draw_info, made_strength)



--- bots/claude_v6/state.py ---
"""
State reconstruction and hand tracking utilities.
"""
from constants import N_PLAYERS, INITIAL_CHIPS, SMALL_BLIND, BIG_BLIND, TOTAL_HANDS
from card_utils import clamp, card_suit, card_number, next_player


def estimate_preflop_strength(my_cards):
    r1 = card_number(my_cards[0])
    r2 = card_number(my_cards[1])
    s1 = card_suit(my_cards[0])
    s2 = card_suit(my_cards[1])
    high = max(r1, r2)
    low = min(r1, r2)
    gap = high - low
    suited = s1 == s2
    pair = r1 == r2

    score = 0.0
    score += (high - 2) / 16.0
    score += (low - 2) / 28.0

    if pair:
        score += 0.25 + (high - 2) / 30.0
    else:
        if suited:
            score += 0.06
        if gap == 1:
            score += 0.06
        elif gap == 2:
            score += 0.03
        elif gap >= 4:
            score -= 0.04

    if high == 14:
        score += 0.04
        if low >= 10:
            score += 0.04

    return clamp(score, 0.0, 1.0)


def preflop_hand_profile(my_cards):
    ranks = sorted((card_number(card) for card in my_cards), reverse=True)
    suited = card_suit(my_cards[0]) == card_suit(my_cards[1])
    pair = ranks[0] == ranks[1]
    return {
        "high": ranks[0],
        "low": ranks[1],
        "suited": suited,
        "pair": pair,
    }


def is_preflop_3bet_candidate(my_cards):
    profile = preflop_hand_profile(my_cards)
    if profile["pair"]:
        return True
    return profile["high"] == 14 and profile["low"] >= 12


def is_preflop_trash_hand(my_cards, preflop_strength=None):
    profile = preflop_hand_profile(my_cards)
    if profile["pair"]:
        return False

    high = profile["high"]
    low = profile["low"]
    gap = high - low
    suited = profile["suited"]
    strength = estimate_preflop_strength(my_cards) if preflop_strength is None else preflop_strength

    if high == 14:
        return False
    if suited and gap <= 2 and high >= 6:
        return False
    if high >= 11 and low >= 8 and gap <= 4:
        return False

    if strength <= 0.30:
        return True
    if not suited and high <= 10 and low <= 5 and gap >= 3:
        return True
    if not suited and high <= 12 and low <= 4 and gap >= 5:
        return True
    if suited and high <= 9 and low <= 4 and gap >= 4:
        return True
    return False


def get_hand_index(req):
    for key in ("hand", "hand_id", "hand_index", "round_id", "round_index", "game_id", "game_index"):
        if key in req:
            try:
                return int(req[key])
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                pass
    return None


def get_remaining_hands(req):
    if "hand" in req and "max_hand" in req:
        try:
            return max(0, int(req["max_hand"]) - int(req["hand"]))
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            pass

    direct_keys = (
        "remaining_hands",
        "remain_hands",
        "hands_left",
        "left_hands",
        "remaining_rounds",
        "remain_rounds",
        "rounds_left",
        "left_rounds",
    )
    for key in direct_keys:
        if key in req:
            try:
                value = int(req[key])
                if value >= 0:
                    return value
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                pass

    hand_idx = get_hand_index(req)
    if hand_idx is not None:
        candidates = [TOTAL_HANDS - hand_idx, TOTAL_HANDS - hand_idx + 1]
        candidates = [value for value in candidates if value >= 0]
        if candidates:
            return max(candidates)
    return None


def infer_remaining_hands_from_requests(requests):
    if not requests:
        return TOTAL_HANDS

    direct = get_remaining_hands(requests[-1])
    if direct is not None:
        return direct

    hand_indices = [get_hand_index(req) for req in requests]
    hand_indices = [value for value in hand_indices if value is not None]
    if hand_indices:
        return max(0, TOTAL_HANDS - max(hand_indices))

    started_hands = 0
    for req in requests:
        if len(req.get("public_cards", [])) == 0 and len(req.get("history", [])) == 0:
            started_hands += 1
    if started_hands <= 0:
        return TOTAL_HANDS
    return max(0, TOTAL_HANDS - started_hands + 1)


def reconstruct_state(req):
    my_id = req["my_id"]
    dealer_id = req["dealer_id"]

    stacks = [INITIAL_CHIPS] * N_PLAYERS
    committed = [0] * N_PLAYERS
    sb = next_player(dealer_id, 1)
    bb = next_player(dealer_id, 2)

    stacks[sb] -= SMALL_BLIND
    stacks[bb] -= BIG_BLIND
    committed[sb] += SMALL_BLIND
    committed[bb] += BIG_BLIND

    current_round = 0
    round_bet = BIG_BLIND
    judge_round_raise = BIG_BLIND
    round_contrib = [0] * N_PLAYERS
    round_contrib[sb] = SMALL_BLIND
    round_contrib[bb] = BIG_BLIND
    alive = [True] * N_PLAYERS
    allin = [False] * N_PLAYERS

    for record in req["history"]:
        record_round = record["round"]
        pid = record["player_id"]
        action = record["action"]
        action_type = record["action_type"]

        if record_round != current_round:
            current_round = record_round
            round_bet = 0
            judge_round_raise = SMALL_BLIND
            round_contrib = [0] * N_PLAYERS

        if action_type == "fold":
            alive[pid] = False
            continue

        if not alive[pid] or allin[pid]:
            continue

        if action_type == "allin":
            add = stacks[pid]
            stacks[pid] = 0
            committed[pid] += add
            round_contrib[pid] += add
            allin[pid] = True
            round_bet = max(round_bet, round_contrib[pid])
            continue

        if action_type in ("call", "check"):
            need = max(0, round_bet - round_contrib[pid])
            need = min(need, stacks[pid])
            stacks[pid] -= need
            committed[pid] += need
            round_contrib[pid] += need
        elif action_type == "raise":
            add = max(0, min(action, stacks[pid]))
            stacks[pid] -= add
            committed[pid] += add
            round_contrib[pid] += add
            round_bet = max(round_bet, round_contrib[pid])
            judge_round_raise = max(judge_round_raise, add)

    public_cards = len(req["public_cards"])
    round_idx = 0 if public_cards == 0 else 1 if public_cards == 3 else 2 if public_cards == 4 else 3

    if current_round != round_idx:
        round_bet = 0
        judge_round_raise = SMALL_BLIND
        round_contrib = [0] * N_PLAYERS

    player_bets = [0] * N_PLAYERS
    for pid in range(N_PLAYERS):
        if not alive[pid]:
            player_bets[pid] = -1
        elif allin[pid]:
            player_bets[pid] = -2
        else:
            player_bets[pid] = round_contrib[pid]

    opponent_id = next_player(my_id, 1)
    opponent_allin = allin[opponent_id] and alive[opponent_id]
    my_round_bet = 0 if player_bets[my_id] < 0 else player_bets[my_id]
    to_call = max(0, round_bet - my_round_bet)
    min_raise_action = max(0, 2 * judge_round_raise - my_round_bet)
    allin_call_amount = max(
        0,
        min(committed[opponent_id], committed[my_id] + stacks[my_id]) - committed[my_id],
    )

    return {
        "round": round_idx,
        "round_bet": round_bet,
        "round_raise": judge_round_raise,
        "judge_round_raise": judge_round_raise,
        "min_raise_action": min_raise_action,
        "round_contrib": round_contrib,
        "player_bets": player_bets,
        "stacks": stacks,
        "committed": committed,
        "pot": committed[0] + committed[1],
        "to_call": to_call,
        "opponent_allin": opponent_allin,
        "allin_call_amount": allin_call_amount,
        "my_round_bet": my_round_bet,
    }


def forced_fold_loss_bound(req, state, my_id, remaining_hands):
    if remaining_hands is None or remaining_hands <= 0:
        return None

    loss = state["committed"][my_id]
    current_dealer = req["dealer_id"]
    for offset in range(1, remaining_hands):
        future_dealer = next_player(current_dealer, offset)
        future_sb = next_player(future_dealer, 1)
        future_bb = next_player(future_dealer, 2)
        if my_id == future_sb:
            loss += SMALL_BLIND
        elif my_id == future_bb:
            loss += BIG_BLIND
    return loss


def collect_latest_requests_by_hand(requests):
    latest = {}
    fallback_hand = TOTAL_HANDS
    for req in requests:
        hand = get_hand_index(req)
        if hand is None:
            hand = fallback_hand
            fallback_hand += 1
        prev = latest.get(hand)
        if prev is None or len(req.get("history", [])) >= len(prev.get("history", [])):
            latest[hand] = req
    return [latest[hand] for hand in sorted(latest)]


=============================
[CLAUDE OUTPUT]
Now here is the JSON output with my analysis and worker assignments:

```json
{
  "analysis": "claude_v6 is Rank 1 overall (ELO 1533.2) but critically loses to claude_v2 at only 0.2 win rate. I studied claude_v2's strategy.py, opponent.py, postflop.py, and simulation.py in depth. The problem is ALGORITHMIC, not just parameter thresholds. claude_v6 is missing 5 major features that claude_v2 has: (1) NO dedicated bb_vs_raise or sb_vs_reraise preflop handling — when v2 raises preflop, v6 has zero 3bet/4bet logic and falls through to generic decision-making; (2) realized_postflop_equity is too optimistic — v6 uses air EQR 0.62 OOP vs v2's 0.56, marginal pair 0.78 vs 0.73, and v6 lacks big-pot/OOP-draw/double-barrel discounts; (3) v2 tracks cbet_rate and fold_to_cbet in opponent model while v6 does not; (4) v2 has concept drift detection while v6 uses static all-time averages; (5) v2 has a safe exploitation framework (exploit_lambda) that prevents over-committing to reads. On the parameter side, the thin_cap in choose_raise is catastrophically low at 0.30 vs v2's 0.46+, causing systematic underbetting of thin value hands. The fix requires BOTH algorithmic additions (Direction A) and parameter corrections (Direction B).",

  "new_experience": "1) Preflop 3bet/4bet logic is essential — without it, aggressive opponents like v2 exploit the hole for massive win rate advantage. 2) thin_cap=0.30 in choose_raise was far too conservative, causing systematic thin value underbetting — v2 uses 0.46+. 3) Realized equity must be more aggressively discounted OOP, facing double barrels, and in big pots. 4) CBet tracking and drift detection in opponent modeling are important for adapting mid-match. 5) Safe exploitation frameworks (interpolating between GTO baseline and exploit) prevent over-adjusting to noise in early hands.",
```

[MASTER (Try 1) PROMPT]
=============================
# Role
You are the Master Bot Architect for a Texas Hold'em poker AI. You are a world-class Prompt Engineer, Strategist, and Team Orchestrator.

# CRITICAL: Tool Usage Rules
You have Read and Bash tools available. When you need to read files or run commands:
- **Use the Read tool** to read local files. Example: Read `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json`
- **Use the Bash tool** to run git commands. Example: `git log --oneline -10`
- **NEVER use webReader or web-search tools** — they cannot access local files and will always fail.
- **NEVER use file:// URLs or GitHub URLs** — all files are on the local filesystem, use Read tool directly.

# Essential Data Files
Read these files FIRST using the Read tool to understand the current state:
- `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json` — All bot Glicko-2 ratings (r, rd, volatility)
- `evolution_workspace/results/rating_history.jsonl` — Rating snapshots over time (trend analysis)
- `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` — Accumulated strategic lessons from past generations
- `bots/claude_v{N}/` — Bot source code directories

Use Bash tool with `git log` and `git diff` to understand evolution history.

# Task
Your goal is to:
1. Read the ratings data and analyze the current bot's performance. Understand the rating trend.
2. Read the experience pool to learn from past iterations.
3. Read the current bot's source code and reference bots' code to identify weaknesses.
4. Update the Experience Pool by editing `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` directly:
   - Add new insights at the bottom.
   - Remove or consolidate redundant entries.
   - Keep the file concise (under 100 lines).
5. Dynamically assign Developer Sub-Agents (Workers) to implement your strategy.
6. You MUST STRICTLY divide your tasks into TWO distinct directions:
   - **Direction A (Algorithmic Logic Architect):** Method refactoring, logic additions, new evaluation functions, or fusing algorithms from reference bots.
   - **Direction B (Hyperparameter Tuner):** FORBIDDEN from altering logic. Only tune constants, thresholds, and magic numbers.
7. Write the exact, comprehensive prompt (`worker_prompt`) for each worker.

# Stagnation Decision
No stagnation detected. Continue from latest version.
If stagnation is detected, you can:
1. Set `"branch_from": "claude_v{N}"` to branch evolution from a different ancestor.
2. Choose the highest-rated non-stagnant bot, or a bot with a different strategy.
3. If no `branch_from` is set, evolution continues from the latest version.

# Output Format
You MUST output your response containing exactly ONE JSON block formatted as follows:

```json

    },

      "difficulty": "easy",
      "worker_prompt": "You are a [Hyperparameter Tuner]. DO NOT change algorithmic flow. Your goal is to tweak the thresholds... [Provide specific constants to adjust and why]"
    }
  ]
}
```

The `branch_from` field is OPTIONAL. Only include it if you want to override the default evolution source.

# Git Commands (use Bash tool)
Run these with the Bash tool:
- `git log --oneline --decorate -20` — See recent evolution history and tags
- `git tag -l "bot-v*"` — List all bot version tags
- `git show bot-v{N}:bots/claude_v{N}/main.py` — Inspect specific past bot code
- `git diff bot-v{A} bot-v{B} -- bots/` — Compare two bot versions

1. Output strictly valid JSON.

6. **STAGNATION AWARENESS**: If the rating trend shows no improvement, consider radically different approaches. Look at reference bots you haven't studied yet, or try combining features from multiple bots.

Add river overbet bluff check (stub, will return None).\n5. Apply anti-bot4 adjustments to thresholds: `strong -= anti_bot4[\"call_threshold_delta\"]`, `medium -= anti_bot4[\"call_threshold_delta\"] * 0.8`, `strong += anti_bot4[\"trap_defense_delta\"]`\n6. Pass `anti_bot4_bonus=anti_bot4[\"raise_size_bonus\"]` and `allow_river_overbet=anti_bot4[\"river_overbet_enabled\"]` to all choose_raise calls.\n7. Modify bluff thresholds: `river_bluff_threshold -= anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]`, `probe_fold_threshold -= anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]`, `semi_bluff_threshold -= anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]`.\n8. Add anti-bot4 bypass to `bad_river_bluff_candidate`: append `and anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"] < 0.05`\n9. Add anti-bot4 bypass to `thin_static_showdown_control`: append `and anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"] < 0.05`\n\n## Task 5: Update allow_low_frequency_blocker_bluff signature (postflop.py)\nAdd `bluff_freq_bonus=0.0` parameter to `allow_low_frequency_blocker_bluff`. In the function body, modify the threshold calculation:\n- Old: `threshold = int(clamp(blocker_profile[\"score\"] * 35.0, 5.0, 18.0))`\n- New: `threshold = int(clamp((blocker_profile[\"score\"] + bluff_freq_bonus) * 35.0, 5.0, 30.0))`\n\nAlso update the call sites in strategy.py to pass `bluff_freq_bonus=anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]` where `blocker_raise` is computed.\n\n## Task 6: Update choose_raise signature (strategy.py)\nAdd `allow_river_overbet=False` parameter to choose_raise. Change the max_ratio line:\n- Old: `ratio = clamp(ratio, low_ratio, 1.45)`\n- New: `max_ratio = 2.2 if (allow_river_overbet and round_idx == 3 and value_profile.get(\"tier\") == \"nut\") else 1.45; ratio = clamp(ratio, low_ratio, max_ratio)`\n\n## IMPORTANT NOTES\n- DO NOT touch constants.py or any threshold values — that's Worker 2's job.\n- DO NOT change the thin_cap formula — that's Worker 2's job.\n- Keep all existing v6 features (drift detection, cbet tracking, realized_postflop_equity with pot parameter, gift_balance tracking, preflop_trash_hand flag) — these are improvements over bot5.\n- Copy bots/claude_v6/ to bots/claude_v7/ first, then work on the copy.\n- After editing, run: `python -c \"import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'bots/claude_v7'); import strategy\"` to verify no import errors."
```json
    },
    {
      "worker_id": 2,
      "role": "Hyperparameter Tuner",
      "target_files": ["strategy.py", "constants.py"],
      "difficulty": "medium",
      "worker_prompt": "You are a Hyperparameter Tuner. You are FORBIDDEN from adding new functions, changing control flow, or modifying algorithmic logic. Your ONLY job is to fix specific numeric constants and thresholds that are proven wrong.\n\n## Context\nThe bot is at bots/claude_v6/. Copy all files to bots/claude_v7/ first (if not already done by Worker 1), then modify ONLY the constants specified below.\n\n## Task 1: Fix thin_cap formula in choose_raise (strategy.py)\nIn the `choose_raise` function, find the thin_cap calculation. Currently it's:\n```python\nthin_cap = 0.46 + 0.08 * wetness + 0.05 * max(0, round_idx - 1)\n```\n\nChange it to the proven bot5 values:\n```python\nthin_cap = 0.30 if round_idx <= 2 else 0.38\n```\n\nAlso remove the `to_call == 0` guard from the thin_control block if present (the thin_cap should apply regardless of to_call). The check should be:\n```python\nif value_plan.get(\"thin_control\", False) and value_profile.get(\"tier\") != \"nut\":\n    thin_cap = 0.30 if round_idx <= 2 else 0.38\n    ratio = min(ratio, thin_cap)\n```\n\n## Task 2: Increase simulation counts (constants.py)\nCurrent SIMULATIONS_BY_PUBLIC_COUNT:\n```python\nSIMULATIONS_BY_PUBLIC_COUNT = {\n    0: 400,\n    3: 800,\n    4: 900,\n    5: 0,\n}\nEXTRA_SIMULATIONS_BY_PUBLIC_COUNT = {\n    0: 200,\n    3: 280,\n    4: 180,\n}\n```\n\nChange to bot5's higher counts for better equity estimation:\n```python\nSIMULATIONS_BY_PUBLIC_COUNT = {\n    0: 600,\n    3: 900,\n    4: 1200,\n    5: 0,\n}\nEXTRA_SIMULATIONS_BY_PUBLIC_COUNT = {\n    0: 300,\n    3: 400,\n    4: 300,\n}\n```\n\n## Task 3: Fix opponent model priors in opponent.py (if Worker 1 hasn't already)\nCheck if the opponent.py smooth_rate priors match bot5's values:\n- VPIP prior: should be 0.58 with weight 4.0 (v6 has 0.52 — too low)\n- PFR prior: should be 0.28 with weight 4.0 (v6 has 0.24 — too low)\n- confidence divisor: should be 35.0 not 30.0 in `clamp((total_actions - 5) / 35.0, 0.0, 1.0)`\n\nIf Worker 1 has already updated opponent.py with bot5's version, skip this task.\n\n## Task 4: Fix realized_postflop_equity base values (strategy.py)\nIn `realized_postflop_equity`, the air_hand eqr values differ from bot5:\n- v6 OOP air: 0.56 (bot5: 0.62) — too pessimistic\n- v6 IP air: 0.68 (bot5: 0.72) — too pessimistic\n- v6 weak pair OOP: 0.73 (bot5: 0.78) — too pessimistic\n- v6 weak pair IP: 0.84 (bot5: 0.86) — slightly low\n- v6 air lower bound: 0.40 (bot5: 0.45)\n- v6 weak pair lower bound: 0.60 (bot5: 0.65)\n\nAlso, v6 adds `big_pot` parameter checks that bot5 doesn't have. Keep those — they're improvements — but fix the base values to match bot5's more optimistic (and proven) numbers.\n\n## IMPORTANT NOTES\n- DO NOT add new functions, imports, or control flow.\n- DO NOT modify postflop.py or tournament.py.\n- DO NOT change the choose_raise ratio calculations or bluff threshold formulas.\n- Only change the specific numeric constants listed above.\n- After editing, run: `python -c \"import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'bots/claude_v7'); import constants; import strategy\"` to verify no syntax errors."
    }
  ]
}
```

# Role
You are the Master Bot Architect for a Texas Hold'em poker AI. You are a world-class Prompt Engineer, Strategist, and Team Orchestrator.

# CRITICAL: Tool Usage Rules
You have Read and Bash tools available. When you need to read files or run commands:
- **Use the Read tool** to read local files. Example: Read `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json`
- **Use the Bash tool** to run git commands. Example: `git log --oneline -10`
- **NEVER use webReader or web-search tools** — they cannot access local files and will always fail.
- **NEVER use file:// URLs or GitHub URLs** — all files are on the local filesystem, use Read tool directly.

# Essential Data Files
Read these files FIRST using the Read tool to understand the current state:
- `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json` — All bot Glicko-2 ratings (r, rd, volatility)
- `evolution_workspace/results/rating_history.jsonl` — Rating snapshots over time (trend analysis)
- `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` — Accumulated strategic lessons from past generations
- `bots/claude_v{N}/` — Bot source code directories

Use Bash tool with `git log` and `git diff` to understand evolution history.

# Task
Your goal is to:
1. Read the ratings data and analyze the current bot's performance. Understand the rating trend.
2. Read the experience pool to learn from past iterations.
3. Read the current bot's source code and reference bots' code to identify weaknesses.
4. Update the Experience Pool by editing `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` directly:
   - Add new insights at the bottom.
   - Remove or consolidate redundant entries.
   - Keep the file concise (under 100 lines).
5. Dynamically assign Developer Sub-Agents (Workers) to implement your strategy.
6. You MUST STRICTLY divide your tasks into TWO distinct directions:
   - **Direction A (Algorithmic Logic Architect):** Method refactoring, logic additions, new evaluation functions, or fusing algorithms from reference bots.
   - **Direction B (Hyperparameter Tuner):** FORBIDDEN from altering logic. Only tune constants, thresholds, and magic numbers.
7. Write the exact, comprehensive prompt (`worker_prompt`) for each worker.

# Stagnation Decision
No stagnation detected. Continue from latest version.
If stagnation is detected, you can:
1. Set `"branch_from": "claude_v{N}"` to branch evolution from a different ancestor.
2. Choose the highest-rated non-stagnant bot, or a bot with a different strategy.
3. If no `branch_from` is set, evolution continues from the latest version.

# Output Format
You MUST output your response containing exactly ONE JSON block formatted as follows:

```json
{
  "analysis": "Your strategic analysis. Which reference bot did you study? What did you learn? Are we failing due to bad logic or bad parameter thresholds?",
  "branch_from": "claude_v{N}",
  "tasks": [
    {
      "worker_id": 1,
      "role": "Algorithmic Logic Architect",
      "target_files": ["strategy.py", "postflop.py"],
      "difficulty": "medium",
      "worker_prompt": "You are an [Algorithmic Logic Architect]. Your goal is to structurally rewrite... [Provide detailed logic instructions, reference which bot to learn from]"
    },
    {
      "worker_id": 2,
      "role": "Hyperparameter Tuner",
      "target_files": ["constants.py"],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "worker_prompt": "You are a [Hyperparameter Tuner]. DO NOT change algorithmic flow. Your goal is to tweak the thresholds... [Provide specific constants to adjust and why]"
    }
  ]
}
```

The `branch_from` field is OPTIONAL. Only include it if you want to override the default evolution source.

# Git Commands (use Bash tool)
Run these with the Bash tool:
- `git log --oneline --decorate -20` — See recent evolution history and tags
- `git tag -l "bot-v*"` — List all bot version tags
- `git show bot-v{N}:bots/claude_v{N}/main.py` — Inspect specific past bot code
- `git diff bot-v{A} bot-v{B} -- bots/` — Compare two bot versions

# Critical Rules
1. Output strictly valid JSON.
2. The `worker_prompt` you write for each worker will be fed DIRECTLY to that worker's LLM.
3. Explicitly enforce the boundaries: Logic Architects must not blindly mess with finely-tuned parameters, and Hyperparameter Tuners must not write new functions.
4. **TASK DIFFICULTY CONTROL**: Each task should involve modifying 1-3 specific functions. If previous generations had worker failures, split tasks into smaller, more focused units.
5. **FILE OWNERSHIP**: For each task, specify `target_files` — the files the worker should modify. Workers must NOT modify files outside their assigned `target_files`.
6. **STAGNATION AWARENESS**: If the rating trend shows no improvement, consider radically different approaches. Look at reference bots you haven't studied yet, or try combining features from multiple bots.

4. Update the Experience Pool by editing `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` directly:
   - Add new insights at the bottom.
   - Remove or consolidate redundant entries.
   - Keep the file concise (under 100 lines).
5. Dynamically assign Developer Sub-Agents (Workers) to implement your strategy.
6. You MUST STRICTLY divide your tasks into TWO distinct directions:
   - **Direction A (Algorithmic Logic Architect):** Method refactoring, logic additions, new evaluation functions, or fusing algorithms from reference bots.
   - **Direction B (Hyperparameter Tuner):** FORBIDDEN from altering logic. Only tune constants, thresholds, and magic numbers.
7. Write the exact, comprehensive prompt (`worker_prompt`) for each worker.

# Stagnation Decision
No stagnation detected. Continue from latest version.
If stagnation is detected, you can:
1. Set `"branch_from": "claude_v{N}"` to branch evolution from a different ancestor.
2. Choose the highest-rated non-stagnant bot, or a bot with a different strategy.
3. If no `branch_from` is set, evolution continues from the latest version.

# Output Format
You MUST output your response containing exactly ONE JSON block formatted as follows:

```json
{
  "analysis": "Your strategic analysis. Which reference bot did you study? What did you learn? Are we failing due to bad logic or bad parameter thresholds?",
  "branch_from": "claude_v{N}",
  "tasks": [
    {
      "worker_id": 1,
      "role": "Algorithmic Logic Architect",
      "target_files": ["strategy.py", "postflop.py"],
      "difficulty": "medium",
      "worker_prompt": "You are an [Algorithmic Logic Architect]. Your goal is to structurally rewrite... [Provide detailed logic instructions, reference which bot to learn from]"
    },
    {
      "worker_id": 2,
      "role": "Hyperparameter Tuner",
      "target_files": ["constants.py"],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "worker_prompt": "You are a [Hyperparameter Tuner]. DO NOT change algorithmic flow. Your goal is to tweak the thresholds... [Provide specific constants to adjust and why]"
    }
  ]
}
```

The `branch_from` field is OPTIONAL. Only include it if you want to override the default evolution source.

# Git Commands (use Bash tool)
Run these with the Bash tool:
- `git log --oneline --decorate -20` — See recent evolution history and tags
- `git tag -l "bot-v*"` — List all bot version tags
- `git show bot-v{N}:bots/claude_v{N}/main.py` — Inspect specific past bot code
- `git diff bot-v{A} bot-v{B} -- bots/` — Compare two bot versions

# Critical Rules
1. Output strictly valid JSON.
2. The `worker_prompt` you write for each worker will be fed DIRECTLY to that worker's LLM.
3. Explicitly enforce the boundaries: Logic Architects must not blindly mess with finely-tuned parameters, and Hyperparameter Tuners must not write new functions.
4. **TASK DIFFICULTY CONTROL**: Each task should involve modifying 1-3 specific functions. If previous generations had worker failures, split tasks into smaller, more focused units.
5. **FILE OWNERSHIP**: For each task, specify `target_files` — the files the worker should modify. Workers must NOT modify files outside their assigned `target_files`.
6. **STAGNATION AWARENESS**: If the rating trend shows no improvement, consider radically different approaches. Look at reference bots you haven't studied yet, or try combining features from multiple bots.

Current evolution: v6 → v7
Bot directory: bots/claude_v6/
Ratings file: evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json
Rating history: evolution_workspace/results/rating_history.jsonl

# Role
You are the Master Bot Architect for a Texas Hold'em poker AI. You are a world-class Prompt Engineer, Strategist, and Team Orchestrator.

# CRITICAL: Tool Usage Rules
You have Read and Bash tools available. When you need to read files or run commands:
- **Use the Read tool** to read local files. Example: Read `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json`
- **Use the Bash tool** to run git commands. Example: `git log --oneline -10`
- **NEVER use webReader or web-search tools** — they cannot access local files and will always fail.
- **NEVER use file:// URLs or GitHub URLs** — all files are on the local filesystem, use Read tool directly.

# Essential Data Files
Read these files FIRST using the Read tool to understand the current state:
- `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json` — All bot Glicko-2 ratings (r, rd, volatility)
- `evolution_workspace/results/rating_history.jsonl` — Rating snapshots over time (trend analysis)
- `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` — Accumulated strategic lessons from past generations
- `bots/claude_v{N}/` — Bot source code directories

Use Bash tool with `git log` and `git diff` to understand evolution history.

# Task
Your goal is to:
1. Read the ratings data and analyze the current bot's performance. Understand the rating trend.
2. Read the experience pool to learn from past iterations.
3. Read the current bot's source code and reference bots' code to identify weaknesses.
4. Update the Experience Pool by editing `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` directly:
   - Add new insights at the bottom.
   - Remove or consolidate redundant entries.
   - Keep the file concise (under 100 lines).
5. Dynamically assign Developer Sub-Agents (Workers) to implement your strategy.
6. You MUST STRICTLY divide your tasks into TWO distinct directions:
   - **Direction A (Algorithmic Logic Architect):** Method refactoring, logic additions, new evaluation functions, or fusing algorithms from reference bots.
   - **Direction B (Hyperparameter Tuner):** FORBIDDEN from altering logic. Only tune constants, thresholds, and magic numbers.
7. Write the exact, comprehensive prompt (`worker_prompt`) for each worker.

# Stagnation Decision
No stagnation detected. Continue from latest version.
If stagnation is detected, you can:
1. Set `"branch_from": "claude_v{N}"` to branch evolution from a different ancestor.
2. Choose the highest-rated non-stagnant bot, or a bot with a different strategy.
3. If no `branch_from` is set, evolution continues from the latest version.

# Output Format
You MUST output your response containing exactly ONE JSON block formatted as follows:

```json

      "difficulty": "easy",
      "worker_prompt": "You are a [Hyperparameter Tuner]. DO NOT change algorithmic flow. Your goal is to tweak the thresholds... [Provide specific constants to adjust and why]"
    }
  ]
}
```

The `branch_from` field is OPTIONAL. Only include it if you want to override the default evolution source.

handles that).\n- DO NOT modify tournament.py, state.py, constants.py, simulation.py, card_utils.py.\n- DO NOT change the cbet/drift detection logic in opponent.py or any references to it.\n- Read editing, verify BOTH files compile:\n  - `cd bots/claude_v7 && python -c 'import strategy'`\n  - `cd bots/claude_v7 && python -c 'import postflop'`\n- The file is large (~1200 lines). Be careful with indentation. Use the existing code style (4-space indent).\n- **CRITICAL**: The `collect_latest_requests_by_hand` function must be added to the imports from `state` at the top of strategy.py."
```json
    },
    {
```
```json
      "worker_id": 3,
      "role": "Hyperparameter Tuner",
      "target_files": ["tournament.py"],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "worker_prompt": "You are a Hyperparameter Tuner. You are FORBIDDEN from altering any algorithmic logic, control flow, or adding/removing functions. Your ONLY job is to adjust numeric constants and thresholds.\n\n## Context\nWe are evolving claude_v6 → claude_v7. The reference bot `bots/bot5/tournament.py` has slightly different anti-lock pressure constants that have been proven through competition. We need to align v7's constants to match bot5's proven values.\n\n## Task: Tune anti-lock pressure constants in `match_pressure_profile` and `apply_anti_lock_pressure`\n\n### In `match_pressure_profile` function:\n\nFind:\n```python\n    profile[\"threshold_delta\"] = 0.050 * protect - 0.060 * chase\n```\nReplace with:\n```python\n    profile[\"threshold_delta\"] = 0.055 * protect - 0.055 * chase\n```\n\n### In `apply_anti_lock_pressure` function:\n\nFind these 4 lines:\n```python\n    match_profile[\"chase\"] = max(match_profile[\"chase\"], 0.85)\n    match_profile[\"threshold_delta\"] = min(match_profile[\"threshold_delta\"], -0.070)\n    match_profile[\"sizing_delta\"] = max(match_profile[\"sizing_delta\"], 0.16)\n    match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"] = max(match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"], 0.11)\n```\n\nReplace with:\n```python\n    match_profile[\"chase\"] = max(match_profile[\"chase\"], 0.90)\n    match_profile[\"threshold_delta\"] = min(match_profile[\"threshold_delta\"], -0.075)\n    match_profile[\"sizing_delta\"] = max(match_profile[\"sizing_delta\"], 0.18)\n    match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"] = max(match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"], 0.13)\n```\n\n### Rationale\n- **chase 0.85→0.90**: More aggressive chasing when behind in match, better exploitation of opponents playing conservatively.\n- **threshold_delta -0.070→-0.075**: Looser call thresholds in anti-lock spots, preventing over-folding when under pressure.\n- **sizing_delta 0.16→0.18**: Slightly larger bets when chasing, maximizing fold equity.\n- **bluff_delta 0.11→0.13**: More frequent bluffs in anti-lock mode, creating more pressure.\n- **threshold_delta formula 0.050/0.060→0.055/0.055**: More symmetric protect/chase balance.\n\nThese are the exact values used by bot5 (the highest-rated reference bot).\n\n## IMPORTANT RULES\n- DO NOT change any function signatures, add/remove functions, or alter control flow.\n- DO NOT modify any files other than `tournament.py`.\n- ONLY change the 5 numeric constants listed above.\n- Also add `TOTAL_HANDS` to the constants import line (it's imported in bot5 but missing in v6): `from constants import N_PLAYERS, BIG_BLIND, LOCK_WIN_MARGIN, TOTAL_HANDS`\n- After editing, verify: `cd bots/claude_v7 && python -c 'import tournament'`"
    }
  ]
}
```

# Role
You are the Master Bot Architect for a Texas Hold'em poker AI. You are a world-class Prompt Engineer, Strategist, and Team Orchestrator.

# CRITICAL: Tool Usage Rules
You have Read and Bash tools available. When you need to read files or run commands:
- **Use the Read tool** to read local files. Example: Read `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json`
- **Use the Bash tool** to run git commands. Example: `git log --oneline -10`
- **NEVER use webReader or web-search tools** — they cannot access local files and will always fail.
- **NEVER use file:// URLs or GitHub URLs** — all files are on the local filesystem, use Read tool directly.

# Essential Data Files
Read these files FIRST using the Read tool to understand the current state:
- `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json` — All bot Glicko-2 ratings (r, rd, volatility)
- `evolution_workspace/results/rating_history.jsonl` — Rating snapshots over time (trend analysis)
- `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` — Accumulated strategic lessons from past generations
- `bots/claude_v{N}/` — Bot source code directories

Use Bash tool with `git log` and `git diff` to understand evolution history.

# Task
Your goal is to:
1. Read the ratings data and analyze the current bot's performance. Understand the rating trend.
2. Read the experience pool to learn from past iterations.
3. Read the current bot's source code and reference bots' code to identify weaknesses.
4. Update the Experience Pool by editing `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` directly:
   - Add new insights at the bottom.
   - Remove or consolidate redundant entries.
   - Keep the file concise (under 100 lines).
5. Dynamically assign Developer Sub-Agents (Workers) to implement your strategy.
6. You MUST STRICTLY divide your tasks into TWO distinct directions:
   - **Direction A (Algorithmic Logic Architect):** Method refactoring, logic additions, new evaluation functions, or fusing algorithms from reference bots.
   - **Direction B (Hyperparameter Tuner):** FORBIDDEN from altering logic. Only tune constants, thresholds, and magic numbers.
7. Write the exact, comprehensive prompt (`worker_prompt`) for each worker.

# Stagnation Decision
No stagnation detected. Continue from latest version.
If stagnation is detected, you can:
1. Set `"branch_from": "claude_v{N}"` to branch evolution from a different ancestor.
2. Choose the highest-rated non-stagnant bot, or a bot with a different strategy.
3. If no `branch_from` is set, evolution continues from the latest version.

relative_strength: 'strong', 'medium', 'weak', 'draw_heavy'\n    ...\n```\n\nThis enables:\n- Recognizing when the board RUNS OUT badly for our hand (e.g., we had overpair on flop but turn brings straight + flush possibilities)\n- Detecting scare cards that should slow us down\n- Recognizing when to double-barrel vs give up\n\n## Integration Rules\n1. Add your functions AFTER theirs in the file.\n\n## Your Mission: Improve 3 Critical Systems\n\n### System 1: Blocker-Based Bluff Candidate Selection\n**Current problem**: Most bots bluff with random weak hands. Top players bluff with hands that have blocking value — cards that reduce the probability opponent has a strong hand.\n\n**What to implement**: A `select_bluff_candidates()` function:\n```python\ndef select_bluff_candidates(hole_cards, board_cards, opponent_range_weight):\n    # Returns a list of bluff actions with:\n    # - bluff_score: 0.0-1.0 how good this hand is as a bluff candidate\n    # - blockers: list of hands we're blocking (e.g., 'blocking nut flush', 'blocking top set')\n    # - showdown_value: does this hand ever win at showdown?\n    # - recommendation: 'excellent_bluff', 'good_bluff', 'marginal_bluff', 'bad_bluff'\n    ...\n```\n\n**Blocker logic**:\n- If we hold the A♠ on a board with 3 spades, we're blocking the nut flush → EXCELLENT bluff\n- If we hold a K on a K-x-x board, we're blocking top pair → GOOD bluff (they're less likely to have top pair)\n- If we hold a middle card that pairs nothing, we have no blocking value → BAD bluff\n- If we have a hand with SOME showdown value (like bottom pair), prefer checking over bluffing\n\n**Bluff frequency should vary by**:\n- Board texture (bluff more on dry boards where opponent rarely has a hand)\n- Position (bluff more in position — we have information advantage)\n- Street (bluff more on flop, be more selective on turn, very selective on river)\n- Pot size (bluff more in small pots, less in big pots where opponent is pot-committed)\n\n### System 2: River Decision Framework\n**Current problem**: River decisions are the most important in poker (most money goes in), but many bots use the same logic as earlier streets.\n\n**What to implement**: A dedicated `river_decision()` function:\n```python\ndef river_decision(hole_cards, board_cards, pot_size, to_call, position, \n                   opponent_model, hand_strength, board_texture):\n    # Returns: action (fold/check/call/raise) and amount\n    #\n    # Logic:\n    # 1. Calculate absolute hand strength on final board\n    # 2. Estimate opponent's range based on all previous actions\n    # 3. Determine if we're value betting or bluffing\n    # 4. Calculate pot odds for calling\n    # 5. Make decision\n    ...\n```\n\n**River value betting tiers**:\n- **Tier 1 (nuts/monster)**: Overbet for maximum value (1.5-2x pot on favorable textures)\n- **Tier 2 (very strong)**: Bet 75-100% pot — target all worse hands that call\n- **Tier 3 (good hand)**: Bet 50-66% pot — thin value, targeting medium-strength calls\n- **Tier 4 (medium)**: Check-call or check-fold depending on opponent\n- **Tier 5 (weak)**: Check-fold, or bluff if we have good blockers\n\n**River calling framework**:\n- Calculate minimum equity needed to call: `needed_equity = to_call / (pot_size + to_call + to_call)`\n- If our estimated equity > needed_equity + safety_margin: CALL\n- The safety_margin should decrease as opponent aggression increases (exploit overaggressive opponents)\n- Against passive opponents who rarely bluff, fold more\n- Against aggressive opponents, call wider\n\n**River bluffing framework**:\n- Only bluff with hands that have LOW showdown value AND good blockers\n- Bluff size should be polarized: either small (30% pot) to fold out weak hands, or large (100%+ pot) for maximum fold equity\n- NEVER bluff on boards where opponent is unlikely to fold (e.g., when they have a draw that got there)\n\n### System 3: Enhanced Opponent Exploitation\n**Current problem**: Most bots either ignore opponent behavior or use simple VPIP/PFR tracking.\n\n**What to implement**: An enhanced opponent model that tracks and exploits:\n```python\nclass OpponentModel:\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.hands_played = 0\n        self.vpip = 0  # Voluntarily put money in pot\n        self.pfr = 0   # Preflop raise frequency\n        self.aggression_factor = 0  # (raises + bets) / calls\n        self.fold_to_cbet = 0  # How often they fold to continuation bets\n        self.check_raise_freq = 0  # How often they check-raise\n        self.showdown_hands = []  # Hands shown at showdown\n        self.bet_size_tells = {}  # Do they bet big with strong/weak hands?\n        ...\n    \n    def get_exploitative_adjustment(self):\n        # Returns dict of adjustments:\n        # - 'bluff_more': bool - if opponent folds too much\n        # - 'value_bet_thinner': bool - if opponent calls too much\n        # - 'tighten_range': bool - if opponent is very aggressive\n        # - 'widen_range': bool - if opponent is very passive\n        # - 'respect_raises': bool - if opponent is tight-passive\n        ...\n```\n\nKey exploitative strategies:\n- **Against calling stations (high VPIP, low aggression)**: NEVER bluff, value bet thinner, bet bigger with strong hands\n- **Against tight-passive (low VPIP, low aggression)**: Bluff more, steal blinds, cbet frequently\n- **Against aggressive (high aggression factor)**: Trap with strong hands, call down lighter, don't bluff\n- **Against tricky (high check-raise)**: Check behind more with medium hands, bet stronger when betting\n\n## Integration Rules\n1. DO NOT break the existing bot protocol (JSON stdin/stdout).\n2. DO NOT change card representation (integers 0-51).\n3. If Worker 1 has added functions (board_texture, draw evaluation), USE them — don't duplicate.\n4. The opponent model should persist across hands using the `data` field in the bot protocol.\n5. Add new functions AFTER Worker 1's additions to minimize merge conflicts.\n6. Keep everything in single file: `bots/claude_v7/main.py`.\n7. Add clear comments. Ensure syntactically valid Python.\n\n## File Ownership\nYou are ONLY modifying `bots/claude_v7/main.py`. Do NOT modify any other files."
```json
    },
    {
```
```json
      "worker_id": 3,
      "role": "Hyperparameter Tuner",
      "target_files": ["bots/claude_v7/main.py"],
      "difficulty": "medium",
      "worker_prompt": "You are a Hyperparameter Tuner for a Texas Hold'em poker AI bot. Your goal is to OPTIMIZE all numerical thresholds, constants, and magic numbers.\n\n## CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT change algorithmic flow or add new functions.\nYou may ONLY:\n- Change numerical values of constants, thresholds, and weights\n- Add new constant definitions (for clarity)\n- Adjust multipliers and factors\n\nYou may NOT:\n- Add new functions or classes\n- Change control flow (if/else logic, loops)\n- Modify function signatures\n- Add new imports\n\n## Setup\n1. Also read `bots/bot5/main.py` and `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` for context.\n\n## Your Mission: Tune These Categories\n\n### Category 1: Preflop Hand Strength Thresholds\nFind and tune all preflop-related constants:\n- Opening raise range by position (SB vs BB in heads-up)\n- 3-betting threshold (what hands warrant a 3-bet vs just calling)\n- Calling a raise threshold\n- Fold to 3-bet threshold\n- Suggested values to test:\n  - Opening raise: top 70-80% of hands from BTN/SB in heads-up\n  - 3-bet: top 15-25% of hands\n  - Fold to 3-bet: bottom 40-50% of opening range\n\n### Category 2: Bet Sizing Multipliers\nFind and tune all bet sizing constants:\n- Flop continuation bet size: typically 33-50% pot\n- Turn barrel size: typically 50-75% pot\n- River value bet size: varies by hand tier (50-150% pot)\n- Raise size multipliers: 2.5-3x for value, 2.5-3.5x for bluffs\n- Overbet threshold: when to bet >100% pot (strong hands on dry boards)\n- Key insight: Smaller c-bets on wet boards, larger on dry boards\n\n### Category 3: Hand Strength Classification Thresholds\nFind and tune where hand strength values transition between categories:\n- 'monster' threshold: typically 0.85+ (two pair+, sets, straights, flushes)\n- 'strong' threshold: typically 0.65-0.85 (top pair good kicker, overpair)\n- 'medium' threshold: typically 0.45-0.65 (middle pair, top pair weak kicker)\n- 'weak' threshold: typically 0.25-0.45 (bottom pair, gutshots)\n- 'trash' threshold: below 0.25\n\n### Category 4: Bluff Frequency Parameters\nFind and tune bluff-related constants:\n- Base bluff frequency: 20-35% of the time when conditions are met\n- Position adjustment: bluff 1.3x more in position than out of position\n- Board texture adjustment: bluff 1.5x more on dry boards vs wet\n- Street adjustment: bluff 1.5x more on flop, 1.0x on turn, 0.5x on river\n- Size adjustment: bluff frequency should decrease with bet size\n\n### Category 5: Pot Odds and Calling Thresholds\nFind and tune calling-related constants:\n- Minimum equity to call: should be `to_call / (pot + 2 * to_call)` + safety margin\n- Safety margin: 3-8% depending on opponent aggression\n- Implied odds multiplier: 1.2-1.8x for draws (higher for hidden draws)\n- Reverse implied odds penalty: 0.5-0.8x for dominated hands\n\n### Category 6: Monte Carlo Simulation Parameters\nFind and tune simulation constants:\n- Number of opponent hand samples: 200-500 (more = more accurate but slower)\n- Number of community card samples: 50-200 per opponent hand\n- Timeout budget: ensure total calculation stays under 2 seconds\n- Weight recent opponent actions more heavily: decay factor 0.85-0.95\n\n### Category 7: Opponent Modeling Weights\nFind and tune opponent modeling constants:\n- VPIP window size: 10-20 hands for initial classification\n- Aggression factor thresholds: passive < 1.0, neutral 1.0-2.0, aggressive > 2.0\n- Fold to cbet threshold: tight < 40%, average 40-60%, station > 60%\n- Weight of showdown evidence vs statistical evidence: 2:1 to 4:1\n\n### Category 8: Position and Stack Depth Weights\nFind and tune:\n- Position advantage multiplier: 1.1-1.3x for in-position decisions\n- Stack-to-pot ratio (SPR) adjustments:\n  - SPR < 3: commit with any pair+ draw\n  - SPR 3-6: standard play\n  - SPR > 6: more cautious, pot control with medium hands\n- Blind defense range: top 50-65% of hands\n\n## Tuning Methodology\n1. Identify EVERY constant/threshold in the file\n2. For each constant, determine if it's reasonable based on poker theory\n3. Adjust values toward optimal ranges listed above\n4. Use consistent naming: `PREFLOP_OPEN_RANGE`, `CBET_SIZE_DRY_BOARD`, etc.\n5. Group all constants at the top of the file or in a dedicated constants section\n6. Add comments explaining what each constant does and its valid range\n\n## File Ownership\nYou are ONLY modifying `bots/claude_v7/main.py`. Do NOT modify any other files."
```

# Role
You are the Master Bot Architect for a Texas Hold'em poker AI. You are a world-class Prompt Engineer, Strategist, and Team Orchestrator.

# CRITICAL: Tool Usage Rules
You have Read and Bash tools available. When you need to read files or run commands:
- **Use the Read tool** to read local files. Example: Read `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json`
- **Use the Bash tool** to run git commands. Example: `git log --oneline -10`
- **NEVER use webReader or web-search tools** — they cannot access local files and will always fail.
- **NEVER use file:// URLs or GitHub URLs** — all files are on the local filesystem, use Read tool directly.

# Essential Data Files
Read these files FIRST using the Read tool to understand the current state:
- `evolution_workspace/results/glicko_ratings.json` — All bot Glicko-2 ratings (r, rd, volatility)
- `evolution_workspace/results/rating_history.jsonl` — Rating snapshots over time (trend analysis)
- `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` — Accumulated strategic lessons from past generations
- `bots/claude_v{N}/` — Bot source code directories

Use Bash tool with `git log` and `git diff` to understand evolution history.

# Task
Your goal is to:
1. Read the ratings data and analyze the current bot's performance. Understand the rating trend.
2. Read the experience pool to learn from past iterations.
3. Read the current bot's source code and reference bots' code to identify weaknesses.
4. Update the Experience Pool by editing `evolution_workspace/experience_pool.md` directly:
   - Add new insights at the bottom.
   - Remove or consolidate redundant entries.
   - Keep the file concise (under 100 lines).
5. Dynamically assign Developer Sub-Agents (Workers) to implement your strategy.
6. You MUST STRICTLY divide your tasks into TWO distinct directions:
   - **Direction A (Algorithmic Logic Architect):** Method refactoring, logic additions, new evaluation functions, or fusing algorithms from reference bots.
   - **Direction B (Hyperparameter Tuner):** FORBIDDEN from altering logic. Only tune constants, thresholds, and magic numbers.
7. Write the exact, comprehensive prompt (`worker_prompt`) for each worker.

# Stagnation Decision
No stagnation detected. Continue from latest version.
If stagnation is detected, you can:
1. Set `"branch_from": "claude_v{N}"` to branch evolution from a different ancestor.
2. Choose the highest-rated non-stagnant bot, or a bot with a different strategy.
3. If no `branch_from` is set, evolution continues from the latest version.

# Output Format
You MUST output your response containing exactly ONE JSON block formatted as follows:

Read the FULL file first.\n\nAlso read `bots/bot5/strategy.py` as your reference for all changes below.\n\n## CHANGE 1: Update imports\n\nAt the top of strategy.py, update the import from opponent to include the two new functions:\n```python\nfrom opponent import build_opponent_model, analyze_current_spot, detect_bot4_profile, get_anti_bot4_adjustments\n```\n\n## CHANGE 2: Remove bb_vs_raise and sb_vs_reraise blocks\n\nFind `choose_preflop_spot_action` function. Inside it, find the block starting with:\n```python\nif spot_info[\"preflop_spot\"] == \"bb_vs_raise\":\n```\nThis spans approximately lines 554-597. REMOVE the entire bb_vs_raise block AND the sb_vs_reraise block that follows it. Replace BOTH with:\n```python\n# bb_vs_raise and sb_vs_reraise: let simulation decide (proven better than fixed thresholds)\n```\n\nThe function should now fall through to `return None` at the bottom for these spots.\n\n## CHANGE 3: Fix thin_cap formula in choose_raise\n\nIn the `choose_raise` function, find the thin_cap calculation (around line 450-453):\n```python\nthin_cap = None\nif value_plan.get(\"thin_control\", False) and value_profile.get(\"tier\") != \"nut\" and to_call == 0:\n    thin_cap = 0.46 + 0.08 * wetness + 0.05 * max(0, round_idx - 1)\n    ratio = min(ratio, thin_cap)\n```\n\nReplace with the proven correct formula:\n```python\nthin_cap = None\nif value_plan.get(\"thin_control\", False) and value_profile.get(\"tier\") != \"nut\":\n    thin_cap = 0.30 if round_idx <= 2 else 0.38\n    ratio = min(ratio, thin_cap)\n```\n\nKey changes: (a) removed `to_call == 0` guard, (b) replaced formula with 0.30/0.38 flat values.\n\n## CHANGE 4: Add new params to choose_raise signature\n\nAdd two new keyword parameters to `choose_raise`:\n```python\ndef choose_raise(\n    min_raise,\n    my_chips,\n    my_round_bet,\n    to_call,\n    pot,\n    win_rate,\n    round_idx,\n    spot_name,\n    preflop_strength,\n    has_position,\n    opponent_model,\n    semi_bluff=False,\n    value_profile=None,\n    value_plan=None,\n    board_texture=None,\n    draw_info=None,\n    blocker_bluff=False,\n    probe_mode=False,\n    pressure_line=False,\n    induce_mode=False,\n    nutted_risk_score=0.0,\n    match_sizing_delta=0.0,\n    anti_bot4_bonus=0.0,       # NEW\n    allow_river_overbet=False,  # NEW\n):\n```\n\nAfter the `ratio += match_sizing_delta` line, add:\n```python\n    ratio += anti_bot4_bonus\n```\n\nChange the final clamp from:\n```python\n    ratio = clamp(ratio, low_ratio, 1.45)\n```\nto:\n```python\n    max_ratio = 2.2 if (allow_river_overbet and round_idx == 3 and value_profile.get(\"tier\") == \"nut\") else 1.45\n    ratio = clamp(ratio, low_ratio, max_ratio)\n```\n\n## CHANGE 5: Add choose_overbet_river function\n\nAdd this new function AFTER `choose_raise` but BEFORE `track_opponent_gift`:\n```python\ndef choose_overbet_river(\n    min_raise, my_chips, my_round_bet, to_call, pot,\n    win_rate, value_profile, board_texture, spot_info, opponent_model\n):\n    \"\"\"River overbet: 1.5-2.2x pot with NUT hands only.\"\"\"\n    if value_profile is None or value_profile[\"tier\"] != \"nut\":\n        return None\n    if board_texture is not None and board_texture[\"wetness\"] > 0.35:\n        return None\n    if pot < 400:\n        return None\n\n    pot_after_call = pot + to_call\n    ratio = 1.5 + 0.3 * max(0.0, win_rate - 0.70)\n    if not spot_info.get(\"has_position\", False):\n        ratio = max(1.3, ratio - 0.2)\n    ratio = min(ratio, 2.2)\n    amount = int(to_call + pot_after_call * ratio)\n\n    if amount >= my_chips:\n        return -2\n    amount = min(amount, my_chips - 1)\n    if amount <= to_call or amount < min_raise:\n        return None\n    return amount\n```\n\n## CHANGE 6: Integrate anti-bot4 into get_action function\n\nThis is the most complex change. Inside `get_action()`, you need to:\n\n### 6a. Add detection after opponent model is built\nAfter `opponent_model = build_opponent_model(requests, my_id)` and before preflop spot action, add:\n```python\n    n_hands = len(collect_latest_requests_by_hand(requests))\n    is_bot4, bot4_score = detect_bot4_profile(opponent_model, n_hands)\n```\nNote: `collect_latest_requests_by_hand` is already imported from state.\n\n### 6b. Compute anti_bot4 adjustments after board_texture is computed\nAfter the line that computes `board_texture` (and after all the postflop profiles are computed), add:\n```python\n    # Anti-bot4 adjustments\n    if board_texture is not None:\n        anti_bot4 = get_anti_bot4_adjustments(bot4_score, board_texture, spot_info, round_idx, value_profile)\n    else:\n        anti_bot4 = {\n            \"bluff_freq_bonus\": 0.0, \"raise_size_bonus\": 0.0,\n            \"call_threshold_delta\": 0.0, \"fold_threshold_delta\": 0.0,\n            \"river_overbet_enabled\": False, \"trap_defense_delta\": 0.0,\n        }\n```\n\n### 6c. Add river overbet check BEFORE the postflop decision tree\nRight after the anti_bot4 computation and BEFORE `repeated_raise_trap` or similar guards, add:\n```python\n    # River overbet with nut hands\n    if round_idx == 3 and to_call == 0 and len(public_cards) >= 3 and value_profile is not None and value_profile[\"tier\"] == \"nut\":\n        overbet = choose_overbet_river(\n            state[\"min_raise_action\"], my_chips, state[\"my_round_bet\"],\n            to_call, pot, win_rate, value_profile, board_texture, spot_info, opponent_model\n        )\n        if overbet is not None:\n            return overbet\n```\n\n### 6d. Add anti_bot4 bypass to bad_river_bluff_candidate\nFind the `bad_river_bluff_candidate` definition. Add this condition at the end:\n```python\n        and anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"] < 0.05\n```\nSo it becomes:\n```python\n    bad_river_bluff_candidate = (\n        round_idx == 3\n        and to_call == 0\n        and made_strength >= 0.18\n        and made_strength < 0.40\n        and not (blocker_profile and blocker_profile[\"eligible\"])\n        and not (value_profile and value_profile[\"tier\"] in (\"strong\", \"nut\"))\n        and anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"] < 0.05\n    )\n```\n\n### 6e. Add anti_bot4 bypass to thin_static_showdown_control\nFind `thin_static_showdown_control` definition. Add:\n```python\n        and anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"] < 0.05\n```\n\n### 6f. Add anti_bot4 bluff_freq_bonus to threshold formulas\nFind the river_bluff/probe_fold/semi_bluff threshold lines:\n```python\n    river_bluff_threshold = 0.62 - 0.28 * match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"]\n    probe_fold_threshold = 0.56 - 0.32 * match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"]\n    semi_bluff_threshold = 0.58 - 0.28 * match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"]\n```\nChange to:\n```python\n    river_bluff_threshold = 0.62 - 0.28 * match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"] - anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]\n    probe_fold_threshold = 0.56 - 0.32 * match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"] - anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]\n    semi_bluff_threshold = 0.58 - 0.28 * match_profile[\"bluff_delta\"] - anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]\n```\n\n### 6g. Pass bluff_freq_bonus to allow_low_frequency_blocker_bluff calls\nFind ALL calls to `allow_low_frequency_blocker_bluff(req, my_cards, public_cards, blocker_profile, round_idx)`. Add `anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"]` as the 6th argument:\n```python\nallow_low_frequency_blocker_bluff(req, my_cards, public_cards, blocker_profile, round_idx, anti_bot4[\"bluff_freq_bonus\"])\n```\n\n### 6h. Pass anti_bot4 params to choose_raise calls\nFind ALL calls to `choose_raise(...)` in get_action. Add these two keyword arguments to each call:\n```python\n    anti_bot4_bonus=anti_bot4[\"raise_size_bonus\"],\n    allow_river_overbet=anti_bot4[\"river_overbet_enabled\"],\n```\n\n### 6i. Add anti_bot4 to call threshold adjustments\nFind the section where `strong` and `medium` call thresholds are computed (near `nutted_risk[\"risk\"]` adjustments). Add:\n```python\n    strong -= anti_bot4[\"call_threshold_delta\"]\n    medium -= anti_bot4[\"call_threshold_delta\"] * 0.8\n    strong += anti_bot4[\"trap_defense_delta\"]\n```\n\n## IMPORTANT RULES\n- ONLY modify `strategy.py` in `bots/claude_v6/`.\n- Do NOT modify any other files.\n- Make ALL changes listed above. They are interdependent.\n- Reference `bots/bot5/strategy.py` to verify exact implementations.\n- After all changes, verify the file parses: `cd bots/claude_v6 && python3 -c 'import strategy'`\n- Keep v6's existing code style and structure.
